person.name: Mary Tudor
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Mary Tudor Aldrich (born PLANTAGENT) was born in 1421, in City Of London, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom, to Owen Tudor and Catherine Tudor (born de Valois).
Owen was born circa 1400, in Anglesey, Principality of Wales.
Catherine was born on October 27 1401, in Paris, Ile-de-France, France.
Mary had 10 siblings: Edmond Earl of Richmond Tudor, Tacina Tudor, Margaret Catherine Tudor, Jasper Pembroke Tudor PLANTAGENET, Owen Thomas TUDOR, Jacinta Baroness Grey Of Wilton TUDOR, Catherine TUDOR, Dafydd OWEN, David OWEN and Henry Henry VI King of England King of France of England.
Mary married William Allred De Aldrich (Aldridge) (Allred) in 1440, at age 19 in Yarmouth, Norfolk, England.
William was born in 1421, in Yarmouth, Norfolk, England.
They had one daughter: Alice Aldrich (born Aldrich (Aldridge) (Allred)).
Mary then married William ALDRICH in 1440, at age 19 in Great Yarmouth, Norfolk, England.
William was born in 1421, in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.
Mary then married Sir William of Yarmouth Aldrich in 1447, at age 26.
Sir was born in 1421, in Great Yarmouth, Norfolk, England.
They had 2 sons: William VI Aldrich and George Aldrich.
Mary passed away in 1487, at age 66 in Great Yarmouth, Norfolk, England - My Heritage.

nameMeanings.name: Tudor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Tudur, a derivative of British Celtic Toutorīx, literally ‘people’ + ‘king’. It has sometimes been taken as a Welsh equivalent of Latin Theodorus, from Greek Theodōros ‘gift of God’ (see Theodore ), for which the usual Welsh form is Tewdwr.
Romanian and Croatian: from the personal name Tudor, a variant of Teodor (see Theodore ).

History: The Tudor royal family, English sovereigns from the accession of Henry VII in 1485 to the death of Elizabeth I in 1603, were of Welsh origin, being descended from Owain ap Maredudd ap Tudur, who held lands at Penmynydd in Anglesey.

variants.name: Theodore
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Serge, Henri, Pierre, Alide, Andre, Antoine, Benoit, Berthony, Chantal, Daphnee, Edouard, Guilene.

French and Mauritian (Théodore); West Indian (in Haiti, Martinique, and Guadeloupe Théodore); English and Welsh: from a personal name derived from Greek Theodōros, a compound of theos ‘God’ + dōron ‘gift’, which was relatively popular in the Middle Ages because of its auspicious meaning. The name was borne by several early Christian saints. There was considerable confusion with the ancient Germanic name Theodoric (see Terry ). In Wales, the personal name Theodore was also adopted as an approximation to the Welsh name Tewdwr (see Tudor ). In North America, this surname has absorbed various other European cognates and their derivatives, e.g. Greek Theodorakis , Macedonian Todorovski, and Serbian Todorović (see Todorovic ).

variants.name: Tedder
meanings: English (Surrey): from the Middle English personal name Thedere, Teddere, which is either from the Old English personal name Thēodhere (from thēod ‘people’ + here ‘army’) or from the cognate ancient Germanic personal name Theudher, which seems to have been adopted by the Normans. Alternatively, perhaps it may be an occupational name from Middle English teddere ‘one who teds’, i.e. a person whose occupation was to spread mown grass for drying.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Mary Tudor was born in 1421, in London, England. She had at least 1 son with William Aldrich. She died in 1487, in Yarmouth, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 66.

fr.lifeSummary: Mary Tudor est née en 1421, à Londres, Angleterre. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec William Aldrich. Elle est décédée en 1487, à Yarmouth, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 66 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Mary Tudor è nata il nel 1421 nel London, England. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con William Aldrich. Morì nel 1487, nel Yarmouth, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 66 anni.
